signals.title: NZD/USD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Geopolitical Shift
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5865 remains under heavy pressure as the USD dominates the G8 scorecard while NZD lags. The announcement of a ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah and Ukraine's peace proposal to Russia have triggered a sharp decline in crude oil prices, weighing on commodity-linked currencies. USD 1W avg +0.622% vs NZD -1.008% creates a 1.630% G8 spread, the widest in the complex. USD is further supported by a +150bp carry advantage over NZD and a strong positive economic surprise index (CESI +28). Watch for 0.5850 support; a break targets the 0.5800 handle as geopolitical de-escalation favors USD funding over high-beta commodity FX.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry + Positioning + Vol
signals.text: USD/JPY at 159.97 is testing the critical 160.00 level, which has previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings. Despite the 300bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage sustaining the bid, the options market is pricing in significant downside protection with a 25d RR of -1.15 (moderate put skew). JPY COT LF net short -86,249 represents a crowded carry trade vulnerable to a squeeze if the BoJ signals a hawkish shift at the June 16 meeting. VIX at 15.4 (normal volatility) suggests carry remains the dominant regime until a break of 160.00 or a spike in volatility above 25.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7128 faces a significant positioning-volatility mismatch. COT LF net long +58,041 (76.1% long) indicates a crowded institutional long, while retail sentiment is 70% short, creating a sharp divergence. AUD/USD IV at 17.6% vs HV30 at 8.1% generates a massive +9.5% VRP, signaling the options market is paying an extreme premium for protection. With a negative CESI (-8) and a bull flattener yield curve regime, the risk of a long-unwind toward 0.7050 is elevated if US NFP data surprises to the upside.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Volatility Premium + CB Real Rate Gap
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1617 exhibits elevated hedging demand with IV at 7.1% vs HV30 at 3.8% (VRP +3.3%). The pair is structurally limited by a -1.49% real rate differential (USD +1.23% vs EUR -0.26%), favoring the USD. While the ECB bias is toward a hike on June 11, the DXY vs SPX correlation break (z=+1.77σ) suggests USD funding stress is providing a floor for the greenback regardless of equity performance. Watch the 1.1600 level; a sustained break confirms the bearish 1M trend (-0.93%).
